title: Multilingual Text
description: A multilingual text bundle: preferred labels keyed by language tag, optional alternative labels (each language as an array of strings), optional ALA-LC transliteration variants, and optional original-script representation. Language tag pattern is a pragmatic subset of BCP-47: lowercase ISO 639-1/-3 codes, optionally followed by a four-letter script code (capitalised), an optional two-letter region code, and an optional private-use 'x-...' subtag. Examples: 'en', 'tr', 'ar', 'fa', 'ota', 'ar-Latn', 'ar-Latn-x-alalc', 'tr-Arab' (Ottoman Turkish in Arabic script).

prefLabel.description: Preferred label (skos:prefLabel). At minimum one language is required. Each entry is exactly one string per language tag. The pipeline guarantees that prefLabel always contains at least 'en' or 'ar' for entities derived from Yâkût/Bosworth/Le Strange/Makdisî.

^[a-z]{2,3}(-[A-Z][a-z]{3})?(-[A-Z]{2})?.description: Alternative labels in this language (skos:altLabel). Variants, historical spellings, regional names. Each language carries a deduplicated list.

altLabel.description: Alternative labels keyed by BCP-47 language tag, each holding an array of strings.

^[a-z]{2,3}-Latn(-x-[a-z0-9]+)?$.description: A romanised form. The recommended scheme is ALA-LC; tag with '-x-alalc' to make this explicit (e.g., 'ar-Latn-x-alalc'). DİN 31635 ('-x-din31635') and IJMES ('-x-ijmes') schemes may also appear.

transliteration.description: Transliteration variants keyed by '<source-lang>-Latn' or '<source-lang>-Latn-x-<scheme>'. Includes diacritics. The slug used in public URIs is derived from the ALA-LC variant by stripping diacritics.

originalScript.description: Native-script representation, keyed by BCP-47 language tag including the script subtag. For Arabic, use 'ar' (default Arabic script is implicit) or 'ar-Arab' explicitly. For Ottoman Turkish, use 'ota' or 'ota-Arab'. For Persian, 'fa' or 'fa-Arab'.

^[a-z]{2,3}(-[A-Z][a-z]{3})?(-[A-Z]{2})?.description: A longer descriptive paragraph in this language. Maps to dcterms:description in JSON-LD export.

$comment: BCP-47 conformance is loose by design: we accept lowercase 2-3 letter language code + optional capitalised four-letter script + optional uppercase two-letter region + optional private-use subtag (x-...). Full BCP-47 (including extended language subtags, variants like 'fonipa', private-use main codes) is out of scope. ISO 639-3 codes used: ota (Ottoman Turkish), arb (Standard Arabic; we collapse to 'ar' in practice).
